notes: This document contains only partial financial statement data — specifically Note 23 segmented disclosure pages for March 31, 2021 and comparative March 31, 2020. Balance sheet, accumulated surplus, full remuneration schedule, and other notes are not present in the provided text. Combined financial statements include consolidated adjustments of -$123,455,283 on revenue and -$96,382,024 on expenses suggesting significant inter-entity eliminations. Housing Development segment shows a large surplus of $27,850,323 in 2021 which may be a one-time item. Education expenses split between Adult Education ($8,945,618) and Education ($20,650,548). Economic Development segment had expenses of $92,955,947 significantly exceeding its revenue.
